From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Area where football teams prepare for matches

For a military facility, see [Military training area](/wiki/Military_training_area "Military training area").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Training_ground "Special:EditPage/Training ground") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Training ground"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Training+ground%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Training+ground%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Training+ground%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Training+ground%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Training+ground%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Training+ground%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(October 2008)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

A **training ground** is an area where professional [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") teams prepare for matches, with activities primarily concentrating on [skills](/wiki/Soccer_skills "Soccer skills") and fitness. They also sometimes form part of a club's [youth system](/wiki/Youth_system "Youth system"), as clubs consider it important to have good facilities to aid the development of young players.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Training grounds are usually separate from a team's [stadium](/wiki/Soccer_stadium "Soccer stadium"), as clubs use the facilities to avoid overusing the stadium's [pitch](/wiki/Soccer_field "Soccer field"). However, teams usually train inside the opposing team's stadium on the day before a [European](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") away game, both for the benefit of the [media](/wiki/Media_(press) "Media (press)") and to become familiar with the surface.

## Training ground incidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Training_ground&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Training ground incidents")]

There have been several high-profile incidents, at training grounds, where players have been injured in disputes between teammates.[[2]](#cite_note-2) [Joey Barton](/wiki/Joey_Barton "Joey Barton") was given a suspended prison sentence, on 1 July 2008, for an assault on teammate [Ousmane Dabo](/wiki/Ousmane_Dabo "Ousmane Dabo") on [Manchester City](/wiki/Manchester_City_F.C. "Manchester City F.C.")'s training ground[[3]](#cite_note-3) and [Andy Carroll](/wiki/Andy_Carroll "Andy Carroll") broke teammate [Steven Taylor](/wiki/Steven_Taylor_(footballer) "Steven Taylor (footballer)")'s jaw in a fight.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Gallery

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Training_ground&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Gallery")]

* [![Germany players Arne Friedrich and Bastian Schweinsteiger training, 2005](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/14/Friedrich_und_Schweinsteiger_2005.jpg/120px-Friedrich_und_Schweinsteiger_2005.jpg)](/wiki/File:Friedrich_und_Schweinsteiger_2005.jpg "Germany players Arne Friedrich and Bastian Schweinsteiger training, 2005")

  [Germany](/wiki/Germany_national_football_team "Germany national football team") players [Arne Friedrich](/wiki/Arne_Friedrich "Arne Friedrich") and [Bastian Schweinsteiger](/wiki/Bastian_Schweinsteiger "Bastian Schweinsteiger") training, [2005](/wiki/2005_in_football_(soccer) "2005 in football (soccer)")
* [![USA players training during the 2006 World Cup.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/57/USMNT_World_Cup_training_20060511.jpg/120px-USMNT_World_Cup_training_20060511.jpg)](/wiki/File:USMNT_World_Cup_training_20060511.jpg "USA players training during the 2006 World Cup.")

  [USA](/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_soccer_team "United States men's national soccer team") players training during the [2006 World Cup](/wiki/2006_FIFA_World_Cup "2006 FIFA World Cup").
* [![Manchester United players training at The Cliff, 1992](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a7/The_Cliff_%28oppvarming%29.JPG/120px-The_Cliff_%28oppvarming%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:The_Cliff_(oppvarming).JPG "Manchester United players training at The Cliff, 1992")

  [Manchester United](/wiki/Manchester_United_F.C. "Manchester United F.C.") players training at [The Cliff](/wiki/The_Cliff_(training_ground) "The Cliff (training ground)"), [1992](/wiki/1992_in_football_(soccer) "1992 in football (soccer)")
* [![Lennoxtown, the training ground of Celtic F.C.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/12/Alanmaguirelennoxtown14.jpg/120px-Alanmaguirelennoxtown14.jpg)](/wiki/File:Alanmaguirelennoxtown14.jpg "Lennoxtown, the training ground of Celtic F.C.")

  [Lennoxtown](/wiki/Lennoxtown_training_centre "Lennoxtown training centre"), the training ground of [Celtic F.C.](/wiki/Celtic_F.C. "Celtic F.C.")
